title: MIF-1
description: MIF-1 (Melanocyte-Stimulating Hormone Release-Inhibiting Factor-1, Pro-Leu-Gly-NH2) is a naturally occurring tripeptide originally discovered as a hypothalamic hormone that acts on the brain rather than the pituitary. It functions as an endogenous opioid antagonist and modulates neurotransmitter systems including dopamine, norepinephrine, and GABA, demonstrating antidepressant, anti-stress, and neuromodulatory properties in both animal and clinical studies.

research.title: From MIF-1 to endomorphin: the Tyr-MIF-1 family of peptides
research.summary: Comprehensive review establishing MIF-1 as the first hypothalamic peptide shown to act on the brain rather than the pituitary, demonstrating antiopiate activity and serving as a prototypic molecule for discovering endogenous opioid antagonists.

research.title: MIF-1 is active in a chronic stress animal model of depression
research.summary: Low doses of MIF-1 (0.1 and 1.0 mg/kg) significantly increased activity and decreased stress-related behaviors in chronically stressed rats, demonstrating antidepressant effects comparable to imipramine in this depression model.

research.title: Neurological effects of MIF-1, MSH, and opiate peptides in clinical studies
research.summary: Clinical research documenting the neurological and behavioral effects of MIF-1 in human subjects, establishing its relevance for neuropsychiatric applications.

research.title: An extraordinary relationship involving MIF-1 and other peptides
research.summary: Retrospective review of collaborative clinical work showing MIF-1 was effective in treating human depression with greater efficacy and faster onset of action than traditional antidepressants available at the time of clinical trials.

research.title: MIF-1: effects on norepinephrine, dopamine and serotonin metabolism in certain discrete brain regions
research.summary: MIF-1 at 5 mg/kg increased striatal tyrosine hydroxylase activity by 25% and dopamine levels in multiple brain regions, demonstrating enhanced turnover of dopamine and norepinephrine neurotransmitter systems.

research.title: MIF-1 and Tyr-MIF-1 augment GABA-stimulated benzodiazepine receptor binding
research.summary: Both MIF-1 and Tyr-MIF-1 significantly augmented GABA-stimulated benzodiazepine receptor binding at low doses, indicating interactions between peptide and GABA-benzodiazepine receptor systems relevant to anxiety modulation.

research.title: Biphasic effects of MIF-1 and Tyr-MIF-1 on apomorphine-induced stereotypy in rats
research.summary: MIF-1 showed biphasic dose-dependent effects on dopamine-mediated behaviors, increasing responses at moderate doses but decreasing them at high doses, suggesting complex modulation of dopaminergic neurotransmission.

research.title: Effects of melanostatine (MIF-1) on focal potentials in slices of rat brain cortex
research.summary: MIF-1 demonstrated activatory influence on CNS cellular activity, enhancing focal potential amplitudes and facilitating post-tetanic potentiation, supporting the neurophysiological basis for its antidepressant properties.

sentiment.summary: Limited user discussion with highly specialized interest focused on experimental melanin reduction and anhedonia treatment, with users expressing cautious optimism but uncertainty about efficacy and safety due to minimal real-world experience data.

effects.label: Depression Models:
effects.description: Effective at 0.1-1.0 mg/kg doses in chronic stress models with activity levels comparable to imipramine

effects.label: Inverted U-Shaped Curve:
effects.description: Clinical evidence shows increasing doses can result in decreasing therapeutic effects, requiring careful dose optimization

effects.label: Rapid Onset:
effects.description: Historical clinical trials reported faster onset of antidepressant action compared to traditional medications available at the time

effects.label: Regional Brain Specificity:
effects.description: Effects vary by brain region with notable activity in striatum, cortex, and olfactory areas but not uniformly across all regions

dosage.label: Low Therapeutic Range:
dosage.description: Animal studies suggest 0.1-1.0 mg/kg as optimal for antidepressant effects in chronic stress models

dosage.label: Moderate Range Effects:
dosage.description: 3-5 mg/kg doses show neurotransmitter modulation with increased dopamine turnover and tyrosine hydroxylase activity

dosage.label: High Dose Concerns:
dosage.description: Doses of 10 mg/kg associated with increased stress effects and hyperalgesia, suggesting therapeutic window limitations

dosage.label: Biphasic Dosing:
dosage.description: Evidence suggests careful titration needed as both very low and very high doses may be less effective than moderate ranges

sideEffects.label: High Dose Effects:
sideEffects.description: At 10 mg/kg, MIF-1 may increase stress responses and produce hyperalgesia rather than therapeutic benefits

sideEffects.label: Regional Variability:
sideEffects.description: Effects on neurotransmitter systems vary by brain region, potentially causing unpredictable responses in some individuals

sideEffects.label: Opioid Interactions:
sideEffects.description: As an opioid antagonist, may interfere with pain management or medications acting through opioid pathways

sideEffects.label: Limited Safety Data:
sideEffects.description: Minimal long-term human safety data available, with most evidence from animal studies or older clinical trials

availability.label: Research Status:
availability.description: Primarily discussed in experimental contexts with limited commercial availability as a supplement or therapeutic agent

availability.label: Peptide Synthesis:
availability.description: Can potentially be synthesized or ordered through research peptide sources, but quality and purity controls are uncertain

availability.label: Off-Label Interest:
availability.description: Small community interest for experimental uses including melanin reduction and treatment-resistant anhedonia, but without established protocols
